title: elaeocarpus obtusus
intro: Elaeocarpus obtusus, also known as the Blue Marble Tree or Blue Olive, is a species of flowering plant in the Elaeocarpus genus, native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which are extracted from its leaves and stems. The health benefits of Elaeocarpus obtusus include treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as providing relief from skin conditions and wounds. Herbal preparations of Elaeocarpus obtusus include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which can be used to create teas, tinctures, and topical creams for various health benefits.
